title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 88:1
body: Has there — there has indeed — come to you the tiding of the Enveloper? the Resurrection so called because it ‘envelops’ creatures with its terrors.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 88:10
body: in a lofty Garden lofty in a physical as well as an abstract sense

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 88:11
body: in which they will not hear read lā yusma‘u ‘will not be heard’ or lā tasma‘u ‘they will not hear’ any vanity any inane soul speaking drivel;

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 88:12
body: therein is a running spring of water meaning ‘springs’;

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 88:13
body: therein are lofty couches lofty in terms of their essence their size and physical location

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 88:14
body: and goblets vessels without handles set around the edges of the springs prepared for them to drink with

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 88:15
body: and cushions arrayed one next to the other against which they may lean

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 88:16
body: and carpets rugs of velvet-hair spread out.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 88:17
body: Will they the Meccan disbelievers not consider by way of reflection the camels how they are created?

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 88:18
body: And the heaven how it was raised?

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 88:19
body: And the mountains how they were set?

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 88:2
body: Some faces on that day — they faces are used to represent the individuals in both instances — will be humbled abject

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 88:20
body: And the earth how it was laid out flat? and thus infer from this the power of God exalted be He and His Oneness? The commencing with the mention of camels is because they are closer in contact with it the earth than any other animal. As for His words sutihat ‘laid out flat’ this on a literal reading suggests that the earth is flat which is the opinion of most of the scholars of the revealed Law and not a sphere as astronomers ahl al-hay’a have it even if this latter does not contradict any of the pillars of the Law.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 88:21
body: So remind them of God’s graces and the proofs affirming His Oneness. For you are only an admonisher;

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 88:22
body: you are not a taskmaster over them a variant reading for musaytir has musaytir that is to say not one who has been given authority over them — this was revealed before the command to struggle against the disbelievers.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 88:23
body: But he who turns away he who rejects faith and disbelieves in the Qur’ān

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 88:24
body: God will chastise him with the greater chastisement the chastisement of the Hereafter the lesser chastisement being that of this world that of being killed or taken captive.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 88:25
body: Truly to Us will be their return their coming back after death

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 88:26
body: then truly with Us will lie their reckoning their requital which We will never abandon.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 88:3
body: toiling weary tired and exhausted from the chains and fetters

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 88:4
body: roasting read taslā or tuslā in a scorching fire

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 88:5
body: made to drink from a boiling spring one of extremely hot temperatures.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 88:6
body: They will have no food except cactus darī‘ — a type of thorn plant which no animal grazes on because of its vileness —

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 88:7
body: neither nourishing nor availing against hunger.

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 88:8
body: Other faces on that day will be delicate fair

title: Tafsir Al-Jalalayn — English · 88:9
body: pleased by their efforts in the life of the world expended in obedience pleased in the Hereafter upon seeing the reward thereof;
